original: According to legend, English saint Swithun is reburied inside Winchester Cathedral against his wishes, and a terrible storm proceeds to rain for 40 days and 40 nights
City of Jerusalem is captured and plundered by Christian forces during the First Crusade
Pope Innocent III states that Jews are doomed to perpetual servitude and subjugation due to the crucifixion of Jesus
Duke Henrik of Corinthia chosen as King of Bohemia
Albert III, Duke of Saxony defeats the Friesian rebellion
Baglione family massacre at the "Blood Wedding" of Astorre Baglione and Lavinia Colonna in Perugia
Portuguese explorer Pedro Cabral arrives back in Lisbon from his voyage to India, having lost six ships, during which he accidently discovered Brazil
Emperor Charles I bans German national synode
Alexei Chirikov sights land in Southeast Alaska. He sends men ashore in a longboat, making them the first Europeans to visit Alaska
French ambassador recalled from London
American troops under General A Wayne conquer Fort Stony Point, NY
World's first steamboat the Pyroscaphe, built by Claude-François-Dorothée, marquis de Jouffroy d'Abbans, makes its first voyage on the river Saône in France
Parliament of Paris banished to Troyes
Gilbert du Motier, marquis de La Fayette, is named by acclamation colonel-general of the new National Guard of Paris
Zebulon Pike begins his journey to explore the American Southwest
First flat horse race held at English race track Cheltenham on Nottingham Hill
A fire destroys the ancient Basilica of Saint Paul Outside the Walls in Rome
Indian tribes Sioux, Sauk, and Fox sign the Fourth Treaty of Prairie du Chien, giving the US most of Minnesota, Iowa, and Missouri
Great Britain, Russia, Austria, and Prussia sign Quadruple Alliance
John Wisden bowls out all 10 South batsmen in the North vs. South match at Lord's
Natal forms as a British colony separate from Cape Colony
CSS Arkansas vs. USS Carondelet and Queen of the West engage at the Yazoo River
President Davis orders service duty for confederate army
Troop train loaded with Confederate prisoners collided with a coal train killing 65 and injuring 109 of 955 aboard
San Francisco Merchants' Exchange opens
Margarine is patented by Hippolyte Mège-Mouriès for use by the French Navy
Georgia, the last Confederate state, is readmitted to the United States
Manitoba becomes the 5th Canadian province and the Northwest Territories are created and transferred to Canada by the Hudson's Bay Company
Baseball's first official no-hitter: George Bradley of the St Louis Brown Stockings no-hits the Hartford Dark Blues, 2-0
Bandai volcano in Japan erupts for the first time in 1,000 years
President Steyn and General De Law escape Brandwater Basin
First Buddhist temple in US forms in Los Angeles, California
Rijksmuseum opens Rembrandt Hall in Amsterdam
46 inches of rain (beginning 7/14) falls in Baguio, Philippines
British National Health Insurance Act goes into effect
The Austro-German forces launch an offensive along the Eastern Front

The head of German propaganda in the US, Dr Heinrich Albert, loses his briefcase on a New York City subway; an examination of its content reveals extensive network of German espionage and subversion across the US
22.22 inches (56.4 cm) of rain falls in Altapass, NC (state record)
World War I: Second Battle of Marne begins
Ruth ties his record of 29 home runs in a season
First duck-billed platypus publicly exhibited in the US at Bronx Zoological Park in New York City
Italian parliament accepts new constitution
VPRO (Free-thinking Protestant Radio Broadcast) forms
Massacre in Vienna: 89 protesters are killed by the Austrian police
First airport hotel opens in Oakland, California
Continental Airlines commences operations
Dutch Second Chamber agrees to temporarily increase defense budget
Buchenwald Concentration Camp opens
Japanese attack Marco Polo Bridge and invade China
Arthur Fagg becomes the first batsman in first-class cricket history to score double centuries in both innings of a match, with 244 and 202 for Kent against Essex in Colchester, England
Clara Adams is the first woman to complete a commercial round-the-world flight from New York, lasting 16 days and 19 hours
Nazi occupiers seize library of IISG Amsterdam

Physicist Donald Kerst is the first person to accelerate electrons using electromagnetic induction, reaching energies of 2.3 MeV, when his betatron device for particle acceleration becomes operational in Urbana, Illinois
Dutch Jews invoked for "labor camps"
First deportation of Jews from Westerbork, Amersfoort, and Vught detention camps in the Netherlands to Auschwitz extermination camps
Greenwich Royal Observatory in London damaged by WW II flying bomb
British North Borneo Company transfers its rights to modern day Sabah to the British Crown
Alcoholics Anonymous is founded in Britain
Israel bombs Cairo during Arab-Israeli War
US President Harry Truman is nominated for his first full term at the Democratic Convention in Philadelphia Convention Hall in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania
WBTV TV channel 3 in Charlotte, North Carolina (CBS) begins broadcasting
First transatlantic helicopter flight begins
ANC members Walter Sisulu and Duma Nokwe leave South Africa and go overseas under false names
110°F (43°C) recorded at Balcony Falls, Virginia (state record)
First commercial jet transport plane built in US tested (Boeing 707)
KOCO TV channel 5 in Oklahoma City, OK (ABC) begins broadcasting
WBOC TV channel 16 in Salisbury, MD (CBS/NBC/ABC) begins broadcasting
Eighteen Nobel laureates sign the Mainau Declaration against nuclear weapons, later co-signed by 34 more laureates
WNDU TV channel 16 in South Bend, IN (NBC) begins broadcasting
Iharos runs a world record in the 10,000 meters (28:42.8)
Dutch Super Constellation crashes near New Guinea, killing 56
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
The US steel strike of 1959 begins, leading to significant importation of foreign steel for the first time in United States history
UN troops arrive in Congo to help deal with the political crisis after Moïse-Kapenda Tshombé's declaration of independence for Katanga province
Spain accepts equal rights for men and women
Algeria becomes member of Arab League
Netherlands and Indonesia reach agreement over New Guinea
KAIT TV channel 8 in Jonesboro, AR (ABC) begins broadcasting
Barry M. Goldwater (Sen-R-AZ) is nominated for president by Republicans
Athanassiades Novas succeeds Papandreo as Premier of Greece
Mariner 4 sends back the first pictures of Mars
LA Wolves defeat Washington Whips 6-5 in overtime to become United Soccer Association champions
USSR performs a 23-kiloton nuclear test at Semipalatinsk in Eastern Kazakhstan
Commercial air travel between US and USSR begins
France performs nuclear test at Mururoa Atoll
The American soap opera "One Life to Live" premieres on TV
The New Jersey Americans move to Commack and become the New York Nets (ABA)
Cincinnati Red Lee May hits four home runs in a doubleheader
Denmark defeats Italy 2-0 in the first Women's World Cup
American TV news reporter Christine Chubbuck shoots herself live on WXLT-TV in Sarasota, Florida; first person to commit suicide in a live broadcast
Military coup on Cyprus: Archbishop and President Makarios flees
36-hour kidnapping of 26 schoolchildren and their bus driver in California
Morarji Desai resigns as Prime Minister of India
Body of Wendy Caulfield, first Green River victim, found near Seattle
Columbia flies to Kennedy Space Center via Dyess AFB, Texas
Armenian extremists bomb Orly, France, killing 8 and injuring 54
John Poindexter testifies at Iran-Contra hearings
State of siege ends in Taiwan
France performs nuclear test at Mururoa Atoll
Sandhi Ortiz-DelValle is the first woman to officiate a men's pro basketball (USBL) game between New Haven Skyhawks and Philadelphia Spirit
US troops leave northern Iraq
World's first off-shore wind farm begins generating electricity off the coast of Vindeby, Denmark
The Security Council of the UN examine violence in South Africa
Derrick Coleman of the New Jersey Nets is accused of rape in Detroit
Gyula Horn sworn in as premier of Hungary
Israel and Jordan agree to talks in Washington, D.C. on July 25th
Sonia O'Sullivan runs 3,000 meters in 8:21.64
Northern Virginia begins using 540 as new area code
Belgian Air Force C-130 Hercules carrying the Royal Netherlands Army marching band crashes on landing at Eindhoven Airport, killing 34; investigation attributed cause to jet engine bird ingestion
MSNBC begins Microsoft internet-NBC TV
Southern Mexico is hit with a 6.5 earthquake
Jerold Mackenzie awarded $26.6M (later reduced to $625,000) for being fired from Miller Brewing for sexual harassment for relaying a Seinfeld episode to a co worker
Donatella Versace launches her first couture collection for her brother's Versace label a year after his murder

Scientists uncover possible reason for SS Waratah's disappearance 100 years later; The SS Waratah was a luxury steamer which did not reach its destination in 1909, and no trace of the wreckage was ever found
The inaugural game at the Seattle Mariners' Safeco Field held in Seattle, Washington

World record rugby attendance of 108,874 sees New Zealand beat Australia 39-35 in a Tri Nations match at Stadium Australia, Sydney; five tries each with Andrew Mehrtens' goal-kicking (6) being the difference
"American Taliban" John Walker Lindh pleads guilty to supplying aid to the enemy and to possession of explosives during the commission of a felony
A. P. J. Abdul Kalam is elected 11th President of India (results announced 18th July)
Anti-Terrorism Court of Pakistan hands down the death sentence to British-born Ahmed Omar Saeed Sheikh and life terms to three others suspected of murdering Wall Street Journal reporter Daniel Pearl
AOL Time Warner disbands Netscape Communications Corporation, and the Mozilla Foundation is established on the same day
MLB Philadelphia Phillies lose to visiting St. Louis Cardinals 10-2, becoming the first franchise in major American sports with 10,000 all-time losses
A 7.9 magnitude earthquake registers 160 km west of Invercargill, New Zealand, creating a small tsunami
39 pilgrims are killed in a bus crash in Parasi, Nepal
A Russian Soyuz rocket with an international team launches for a mission to the International Space Station
18 people are killed and 47 are injured in a riot following a boxing match in Indonesia
A car bomb in Deir Atiyah, Syria, kills 13 people
20 people are killed and 100 are injured after a train derails in Moscow
Israel announces it has officially voted to accept the ceasefire proposed by Egypt; Hamas rejects it, saying they were not consulted
Nearly 300 people are killed in a failed military coup in Turkey, and 6,000 people are later arrested
Australian Justine Damond killed by Minneapolis police officer after calling 911 for a disturbance
Eight-year-old girl finds pre-Viking-era sword in Vidöstern Lake, Sweden; internet proclaims her Queen of Sweden
Tampa Bay catcher Travis d'Arnaud becomes the first player in MLB history to hit three home runs while catching and batting leadoff in the Rays' 5-4 win over the NY Yankees
Heavy monsoon flooding in India's northeastern state of Assam affects more than 2 million people and kills more than 50
Devastating floods linked to climate change sweep through towns in western Germany, Belgium, and the Netherlands after record rainfall, killing at least 188 people
Elton Johnkicks off the North American leg of his "Farewell Yellow Brick Road: The Final Tour" at Citizens Bank Park, Philadelphia, Pennsylvania
Torrential rain totaling almost 300 mm (11.8 in) across South Korea leads to at least 37 deaths and floods a tunnel in the South Korean town of Osong, trapping 15 vehicles
Kenyan man confesses to the murder of 42 women after the bodies of nine women are found in a Nairobi rubbish dump
King Johnof England expels Canterbury monks for supporting Pope's choice of Archbishop of Canterbury, Stephen Langton
Novgorodian army led byAlexander Nevskydefeats the Swedes in the Battle of the Neva
French marshalJoachim Muratbecomes King of Naples
Napoleonsurrenders to Captain Frederick Maitland of HMS Bellerophon at Rochefort after his earlier defeat at the Battle of Waterloo
Camillo Golgidiscovers the Golgi Apparatus (a delicate network inside cells essential for the transmission and reception of information between cells)
NY GiantChristy Mathewsonno-hits St. Louis 5-0
Detroit's future Baseball Hall of Fame center fielderTy Cobbhits two inside-the-park home runs to lead the Tigers to a sweep of the Washington Senators, 9-5 and 7-0

American athleteJim Thorpeplaces in the top 4 in all 10 events, achieving an Olympic record of 8,413 points to win the Decathlon gold medal at the Stockholm Olympics; his medal is stripped in 1913 (played pro baseball) and reinstated in 1982
Mexican PresidentVictoriano Huertaflees with 2 million pesos to Europe
Boeing Company (Pacific Aero) formed byWilliam Boeingin Seattle, Washington
NY Yankees sluggerBabe Ruthties MLB record of 138 career home runs (held by Roger Connor since 1895) in 7-2 win over St. Louis Browns at Sportsman's Park, St. Louis
US PresidentHerbert Hoovercuts own salary by 15%
Wiley Postbegins the first solo flight around the world, completing the journey in 7 days and 19 hours
Britain's MAUD Report, edited by physicistJames Chadwick, is approved and concludes that an atomic bomb is feasible
Howard Floreyand Norman Heatley present freeze-dried mold cultures of penicillin
PresidentEisenhowersends US troops to Lebanon; they stay for 3 months
Baltimore's future Baseball Hall of Fame third basemanBrooks Robinsongoes 5 for 5, including the cycle in the Orioles' 5-2 win over the Chicago White Sox
Paul McCartneyis fined £17 for speeding
Leonard Bernsteinpremieres his "Chichester Psalms" for chorus and orchestra at the Philharmonic Hall, New York City
US PresidentRichard Nixonannounces he will visit the People's Republic of China
"Honky Chateau" becomesElton John's first No. 1 album in the US, includes hit "Rocket Man"
California AngelNolan Ryan's pitches his second no-hitter, defeating the Detroit Tigers 6-0
MLB San Francisco GiantWillie McCoveybecomes 15th to hit 400 home runs, this one at Candlestick Park against Pittsburgh Pirate's Bob Moose
Ray Daviesannounces his retirement from The Kinks, then attempts suicide; neither effort successful
Soyuz 19and Apollo 18 are launched; will rendezvous 2 days later
British Open Men's Golf at St Andrews:Jack Nicklauscompletes his third career Grand Slam, winning by 2 strokes overBen CrenshawandRay Floyd
Billy Carter, the brother of US PresidentJimmy Carter, registers as a foreign agent of the Libyan government after it was revealed they paid him hundreds of thousands of dollars
Johnny Benchhits his 314th HR as a catcher which breaksYogi Berra's record
Senate confirmsGeorge P. Shultzas the US 60th Secretary of State by a vote of 97-0
Film version of "The Pirates of Penzance," starringKevin Kline,Linda Ronstadt, andAngela Lansbury, premieres in the US
John Lennon's single "I'm Stepping Out" released posthumously
Boy Georgeis barred from a British TV show because he may be a bad influence
"Die Hard," directed by John McTiernan and starringBruce WillisandAlan Rickman, is released in the US
PopeJohn Paul IIhospitalized for 3 weeks to have tumor removed
After 2,216 consecutive games at shortstop, MLB Baltimore Orioles'Cal Ripkenmoves to third base
"Wedding Crashers," starringOwen WilsonandVince Vaughn, premieres
After declining an offer to remain with the Anaheim Mighty Ducks,Mike Babcockis named head coach of the Detroit Red Wings
"Harry Potterand the Half-Blood Prince," the sixth film based on the books byJ. K. Rowling, is released worldwide
Korean pop singerPsyreleases single "Gangnam Style," it becomes a worldwide hit, topping the charts in over 36 countries
Jason Mrazreleases his fifth studio album, "Yes!"
Robbie Williamsannounces he is returning to boy bandTake That
"Stranger Things," created by the Duffer Brothers and starringWinona Ryder, David Harbour,Finn Wolfhard,Natalia Dyer, andMillie Bobby Brown, debuts on Netflix
FIFA World Cup Final, Luzhniki Stadium, Moscow, Russia: Former captainDidier Deschampsbecomes only the third man to win the event as a player and coach as France beats Croatia, 4-2
Filipino boxing legendManny Pacquiaostops WBA welterweight champion Lucas Matthysse in the seventh round in Kuala Lumpur for his first knockout in 9 years and 60th career victory
Argentine footballerLionel Messiofficially signs for Inter Miami for 2 1/2 years, with salary between $50 million and $60 million a year
SingerEd Sheeranwelcomes surprise guestEminemfor two duets during his concert at Ford Field in Detroit, Michigan
Donald Trumppicks Ohio SenatorJD Vanceas his Republican vice-presidential running mate
John Ball, a leader in the Peasants' Revolt, is hanged, drawn and quartered in the presence ofRichard IIof England

Battle of Grunwald (First Battle of Tannenburg, Battle of Žalgiris), one of Medieval Europe's largest battles during the Polish-Lithuanian Teutonic War. Polish King Władysław Jagiełło and Lithuanian Grand Duke Vytautas defeat Teutonic Ulrich von Jungingen.
The Rosetta Stone is found in the Egyptian village of Rosetta by French Captain Pierre-François Bouchard duringNapoleon's Egyptian Campaign
Chubby Checkerreleases his version of "The Twist" in the US (date approximate)
Hundreds of thousands of Hutus flee to Zaire in the Congo near the end of the Rwandan Genocide
Ichijō, 66th Emperor of Japan (986-1011), born in Heian Kyō, Japan (d. 1011)
